cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the answer to the question based on the passage about Isabelle Dinoire. The question is: It can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks that _ . The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to pick out her remarks. First, she was attacked by her dog and had a face transplant. The passage says she saw her face in the mirror two days after the operation. She said, "I was scared to look at myself, but when I did it was already marvelous and I couldn't believe it... it was already beautiful." Then later, less than a year after the 15-hour operation, she's trying to rebuild her life and says, "I have been saved... It's a miracle somehow." So her remarks are positive about the result. Let's look at the options: (A) she was too shocked to see herself in the mirror. The passage says she was scared, but when she looked, she found it marvelous. So she wasn't just shocked; she was positively surprised. So A might not be correct because she wasn't too shocked to look—she did look and felt positive. (B) she was satisfied with the result of the operation. Her comments about it being "marvelous," "beautiful," and a "miracle" suggest satisfaction. So B is likely correct. (C) she thought her damaged face couldn't be repaired. The passage mentions that doctors initially offered little hope with conventional surgery, but Isabelle's own remarks don't indicate that she thought it couldn't be repaired. She was surprised by the positive result. So C might not be correct. (D) she looked more beautiful than before. She said it was beautiful, but the passage doesn't compare it to her appearance before the attack. So D might be an overstatement. She might not have had a
